paper.title: 60 candidate high-velocity stars originating from the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal galaxy in Gaia EDR3

review.summary: This paper identifies 60 candidate high-velocity stars likely originating from the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal galaxy (Sgr) via backward orbit integration using Gaia EDR3 astrometry combined with radial velocities from nine spectroscopic surveys. The internal data assets are: (1) a calibration table (Table 1) listing radial velocity offset and error-correction parameters for each survey relative to Gaia, required to homogenize the multi-survey radial velocity sample; and (2) a candidate catalog table (Table 2, commented out in the source but referenced in the paper) presenting Galactocentric distances, total velocities, traceback times, normalized minimum approach distances, velocity differences relative to Sgr, Sgr-origin probabilities, and unbound probabilities for the 60 stars. An external online catalog of stellar properties is hosted at the China-VO NADC portal. No other internal observational tables were found in the LaTeX source.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the radial velocity offset and error-correction parameters for each spectroscopic survey relative to Gaia, used to homogenize multi-survey radial velocity measurements before orbit integration.

source_refs.caption: Correction parameters for radial velocity and its error.
source_refs.label: tab:correction

columns.name: Survey
columns.meaning: Name of the spectroscopic survey providing radial velocity data.

columns.name: offset | \colheadkm s^-1
columns.meaning: Median radial velocity offset of the survey relative to Gaia.

columns.name: k
columns.meaning: Multiplicative correction factor applied to the survey's radial velocity error.

columns.name: \colhead\sigma_v, floor | \colheadkm s^-1
columns.meaning: Floor value added in quadrature to the corrected radial velocity error.

columns.name: Method
columns.meaning: Correction method used: 'dupl' for duplicate-observation fitting or 'cross' for cross-survey fitting.

internal_tables.evidence: Correction parameters for radial velocity and its error.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents the orbital properties, Sgr-origin probabilities, and unbound probabilities for the 60 candidate high-velocity stars identified as likely tidally stripped from the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal galaxy.

source_refs.caption: Properties of stars that originate from Sgr.
source_refs.label: tab:results

columns.name: ID
columns.meaning: Internal star identifier used in this study.

columns.name: source-id
columns.meaning: Unique source identifier from the Gaia EDR3 catalog.

columns.name: v_GC | \colhead(km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Total Galactocentric velocity of the star.

columns.name: t_trace | (Myr)
columns.meaning: Backward trace time to the closest approach between the star and Sgr.

columns.name: \Delta r_min^*
columns.meaning: Minimum approach distance between the star and Sgr normalized by the Sgr half-light radius.

columns.name: \Delta v_GC | \colhead(km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Velocity difference between the star and Sgr at the time of closest approach.

columns.name: P_Sgr
columns.meaning: Probability that the star originates from the Sagittarius dwarf spheroidal galaxy.

columns.name: P_ub
columns.meaning: Probability that the star is gravitationally unbound from the Milky Way.

internal_tables.evidence: Properties of stars that originate from Sgr.
internal_tables.comments: The table environment is commented out in the source LaTeX; the actual data rows are not present in the archived source.
